iteration_focus: Add mTLS auth support (issue #64). Focused iteration: only the mTLS positive eval (id 1) was run end-to-end against the model, since it's the eval whose expected behavior changed. Other evals are covered by the 78-test script-level suite (test_introspect.sh, all passing) and JSON Schema validation of both example manifests.

comparison.notes: The pass-rate delta is by design: the OLD skill correctly refuses MTLS manifests (that was the v1 contract — Precondition 2 hard-fails on non-SASL_SCRAM auth) and so fails every assertion in the new positive eval. The NEW skill accepts the manifest, generates the kafka-payments/ skill with the right MTLS shape (CONTEXTS_<UPPER>_TLS_ENABLED only, no SASL_*), wires build_cert_mount_args into every wrapper, and surfaces the unreachable-broker smoke-test failure honestly. Tokens / time are higher because the new skill has more work to do (the OLD skill exits at Precondition 2 in a few hundred tokens; the NEW skill walks all six steps end-to-end).
